• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

R Notebook

Load Libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
library(MASS)
library(leaps)

Import the csv file into R

h_info <- read.csv("C:/Users/gfern/OneDrive/Documents/WGU/D600/TASK1/housing_information.csv")

View the file ‘housing_info’

View(h_info)

Find missing values

sum(is.na(h_info))
[1] 0

Inspect column Names

colnames(h_info)
 [1] "ID"                   "Price"                "SquareFootage"        "NumBathrooms"         "NumBedrooms"         
 [6] "BackyardSpace"        "CrimeRate"            "SchoolRating"         "AgeOfHome"            "DistanceToCityCenter"
[11] "EmploymentRate"       "PropertyTaxRate"      "RenovationQuality"    "LocalAmenities"       "TransportAccess"     
[16] "Fireplace"            "HouseColor"           "Garage"               "Floors"               "Windows"             
[21] "PreviousSalePrice"    "IsLuxury"

Inspect unique values

unique(h_info$Windows)
 [1] 13 17 34 14 21 36 10 15 19  9  2 23 11  8 22 16  4 12 20  3 18 45 29 40 50 42 26 28  6 38 25  5  7 51 32  0  1 24 -3 27 31 60
[43] 37 44 -4 41 39 30 46 33 48 35 -2 49 -1 47 43 54 59 52 61 53 56 58 57 55 63 -6 -5

Convert to absolute values

h_info$Windows <- abs(h_info$Window)

Calculate summary for ‘CrimeRate’

mean_crime <- mean(h_info$CrimeRate, na.rm = TRUE)   # Mean
print(paste( "Mean:", round(mean_crime, 2)))
[1] "Mean: 31.23"
      
median_crime <- median(h_info$CrimeRate, na.rm = TRUE)  # Median
print(paste("Median:", round(median_crime, 2)))
[1] "Median: 30.38"
mode_crime <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(h_info$CrimeRate), decreasing = TRUE)[1]))  # Mode
print(paste("Mode:", round(mode_crime, 2)))
[1] "Mode: 34.01"
range_crime <- range(h_info$CrimeRate, na.rm = TRUE)  # Range
print(paste("Range:", paste(round(range_crime, 2), collapse = " - ")))
[1] "Range: 0.03 - 99.73"
min_crime <- min(h_info$CrimeRate, na.rm = TRUE)  # Minimum
print(paste("Minimum:", round(min_crime, 2)))
[1] "Minimum: 0.03"
max_crime <- max(h_info$CrimeRate, na.rm = TRUE)  # Maximum
print(paste("Maximum:", round(max_crime, 2)))
[1] "Maximum: 99.73"
sd_crime <- sd(h_info$CrimeRate, na.rm = TRUE)  # Standard Deviation
print(paste("Standard Deviation:", round(sd_crime, 2)))
[1] "Standard Deviation: 18.03"

Calculate summary for ‘DistanceToCityCenter’

mean_distance <- mean(h_info$DistanceToCityCenter, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Mean:", round(mean_distance, 2)))
[1] "Mean: 17.48"
median_distance <- median(h_info$DistanceToCityCenter, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Median:", round(median_distance, 2)))
[1] "Median: 15.62"
mode_distance <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(h_info$DistanceToCityCenter), decreasing = TRUE)[1]))  # mode calculation
print(paste("Mode:", round(mode_distance, 2)))
[1] "Mode: 8.29"
range_distance <- range(h_info$DistanceToCityCenter, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Range:", paste(round(range_distance, 2), collapse = " - ")))
[1] "Range: 0 - 65.2"
min_distance <- min(h_info$DistanceToCityCenter, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Minimum:", round(min_distance, 2)))
[1] "Minimum: 0"
max_distance <- max(h_info$DistanceToCityCenter, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Maximum:", round(max_distance, 2)))
[1] "Maximum: 65.2"
sd_distance <- sd(h_info$DistanceToCityCenter, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Standard Deviation:", round(sd_distance, 2)))
[1] "Standard Deviation: 12.02"

mean_schoolrating <- mean(h_info$SchoolRating, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Mean:", round(mean_schoolrating, 2)))
[1] "Mean: 6.94"
median_schoolrating <- median(h_info$SchoolRating, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Median:", round(median_schoolrating, 2)))
[1] "Median: 7.01"
mode_schoolrating <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(h_info$SchoolRating), decreasing = TRUE)[1]))  # mode calculation
print(paste("Mode:", round(mode_schoolrating, 2)))
[1] "Mode: 10"
range_schoolrating <- range(h_info$SchoolRating, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Range:", paste(round(range_schoolrating, 2), collapse = " - ")))
[1] "Range: 0.22 - 10"
min_schoolrating <- min(h_info$SchoolRating, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Minimum:", round(min_schoolrating, 2)))
[1] "Minimum: 0.22"
max_schoolrating <- max(h_info$SchoolRating, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Maximum:", round(max_schoolrating, 2)))
[1] "Maximum: 10"
sd_schoolrating <- sd(h_info$SchoolRating, na.rm = TRUE)
print(paste("Standard Deviation:", round(sd_schoolrating, 2)))
[1] "Standard Deviation: 1.89"

Univariate Visualizations for independent variables

ggplot(h_info, aes(x = CrimeRate)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Crime", x = "Crime", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()


ggplot(h_info, aes(x = DistanceToCityCenter)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "lightcoral", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Distance To City Center", x = "City Distance", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()


ggplot(h_info, aes(x = SchoolRating)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "lightgrey", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of School Rating", x = "School Rating", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

Bivariate Visualizations for independent and dependent variables

For ‘DistanceToCityCenter’ and ‘CrimeRate’

ggplot(h_info, aes(x = DistanceToCityCenter, y = CrimeRate , color = CrimeRate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +  # Adjust transparency and point size
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +  # Add regression line
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "lightcoral") +  # Color gradient from light to dark
  labs(title = "Crime Rate and Distance to City Center", 
       x = "Crime Rate", 
       y = "Distance to City Center", 
       color = "Crime Rate") +  # Legend label
  theme_minimal()

For ‘DistanceToCityCenter’ and ‘SchoolRating’

ggplot(h_info, aes(x = DistanceToCityCenter  , y = SchoolRating , color = SchoolRating)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +  # Adjust transparency and point size
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +  # Add regression line
  scale_color_gradient(low = "green", high = "blue") +  # Color gradient from light to dark
  labs(title = "Distance To City Center and School Rating", 
       x = "Distance To City Center", 
       y = "School Rating", 
       color = "School Rating") +  # Legend label
  theme_minimal()

Define the dataset split and select the variables

set.seed(123)  # Set seed for reproducibility
selected_variables <- c("DistanceToCityCenter", "CrimeRate", "SchoolRating")   # Define variables to keep in the dataset
h_info_selected_variables <- h_info[selected_variables]  # Keep only relevant variables

Create an index for training data

train_index <- createDataPartition(h_info_selected_variables$DistanceToCityCenter, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)

Subset the data into training set and testing set

training_data <- h_info_selected_variables[train_index, ]  # 70% Training
test_data <- h_info_selected_variables[-train_index, ]  # 30% Testing

Check the dimensions of the new datasets

dim(training_data)  # Size of training data
[1] 4900    3
dim(test_data)   # Size of testing data
[1] 2100    3

Create a linear regression model

h_info_regression_model <- lm(DistanceToCityCenter ~ CrimeRate + SchoolRating, data = training_data)
summary(h_info_regression_model)

Call:
lm(formula = DistanceToCityCenter ~ CrimeRate + SchoolRating, 
    data = training_data)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-22.876  -9.420  -1.598   7.520  49.260 

Coefficients:
              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  23.660117   0.768851  30.773  < 2e-16 ***
CrimeRate     0.036231   0.009574   3.784 0.000156 ***
SchoolRating -1.055667   0.090986 -11.602  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Residual standard error: 11.84 on 4897 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.034, Adjusted R-squared:  0.03361 
F-statistic: 86.18 on 2 and 4897 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Perform forward stepwise selection using AIC as criteria

forward_stepwise_selection <- stepAIC(h_info_regression_model, direction = "forward", scope = list(lower = ~1, upper = ~CrimeRate + SchoolRating), trace = FALSE)
summary(forward_stepwise_selection)

Call:
lm(formula = DistanceToCityCenter ~ CrimeRate + SchoolRating, 
    data = training_data)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-22.876  -9.420  -1.598   7.520  49.260 

Coefficients:
              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  23.660117   0.768851  30.773  < 2e-16 ***
CrimeRate     0.036231   0.009574   3.784 0.000156 ***
SchoolRating -1.055667   0.090986 -11.602  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Residual standard error: 11.84 on 4897 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.034, Adjusted R-squared:  0.03361 
F-statistic: 86.18 on 2 and 4897 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Perform backward stepwise elimination using AIC as criteria

backward_stepwise_selection <- stepAIC(h_info_regression_model, direction = "backward", trace = FALSE)
summary(backward_stepwise_selection)

Call:
lm(formula = DistanceToCityCenter ~ CrimeRate + SchoolRating, 
    data = training_data)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-22.876  -9.420  -1.598   7.520  49.260 

Coefficients:
              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  23.660117   0.768851  30.773  < 2e-16 ***
CrimeRate     0.036231   0.009574   3.784 0.000156 ***
SchoolRating -1.055667   0.090986 -11.602  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Residual standard error: 11.84 on 4897 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.034, Adjusted R-squared:  0.03361 
F-statistic: 86.18 on 2 and 4897 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Perform both directions (forward and backward) stepwise selection using AIC

bothdirections_selection <- stepAIC(h_info_regression_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE)
summary(bothdirections_selection)

Call:
lm(formula = DistanceToCityCenter ~ CrimeRate + SchoolRating, 
    data = training_data)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-22.876  -9.420  -1.598   7.520  49.260

              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  23.660117   0.768851  30.773  < 2e-16 ***
CrimeRate     0.036231   0.009574   3.784 0.000156 ***
SchoolRating -1.055667   0.090986 -11.602  < 2e-16 ***
---
